revision.text:
[[ja:今井氏]] {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Imai Clan (今井氏) | crest = 井桁に唐花菱.png | crest_caption = {{ruby|Igeta ni Karahanabishi|いげた に からはなびし}} | origin_family = 1. {{ill|Kiso-Nakahara Clan|ja|中原氏 (木曽)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso-Nakahara Clan|1=Nakahara clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q85875426}} (descendants of the Nakahara clan from the Meikyōdō tradition)
2. {{ill|Seiwa Genji|en|Seiwa Genji|qid=Q2164991|lt=Seiwa Genji|1=Seiwa Genji}}, branch of the {{ill|Takeda clan|en|Takeda clan|qid=Q1210114|lt=Takeda Clan|1=Takeda clan}}
3. {{ill|Seiwa Genji|en|Seiwa Genji|qid=Q2164991|lt=Seiwa Genji|1=Seiwa Genji}}, branch of the {{ill|Nitta clan|en|Nitta clan|qid=Q2274150|lt=Nitta Clan|1=Nitta clan}} | founder = 1. {{ill|Imai Kanehira|en|Imai Kanehira|qid=Q3192618|lt=Imai Kanehira|1=Imai Kanehira}} | type = {{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Buke|qid=Q2927809}}
{{ill|Kazoku|en|Kazoku|qid=Q241734|lt=Kazoku|1=Kazoku}} (Viscount) | origin = {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|qid=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}}, {{ill|Chikuma District, Shinano|en|Chikuma District, Shinano|qid=Q5097503|lt=Chikuma District|1=Chikuma District, Shinano}}, Imai Village, or {{ill|Kiso District|en|Kiso District|qid=Q1348570|lt=Kiso District, Nagano|1=Kiso District}}, Imai Village
{{ill|Kai Province|en|Kai Province|qid=Q858076|lt=Kai Province|1=Kai Province}}, {{ill|Yamanashi District|ja|山梨郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi District|qq=}}, Imai Village
{{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|qid=Q1054838|lt=Kōzuke Province|1=Kōzuke Province}}, {{ill|Nitta Shōen|en|Nitta Shōen|qid=Q24897972|lt=Nitta Manor|1=Nitta Shōen}}, Imai Village | main_base = {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|qid=Q1054838|lt=Kōzuke Province|1=Kōzuke Province}}, {{ill|Seta District, Gunma|en|Seta District, Gunma|qid=Q2324302|lt=Seta District|1=Seta District, Gunma}}, Kitakatsushika Village, Hakoda | notable_members = {{ill|Imai Kanehira|en|Imai Kanehira|qid=Q3192618|lt=Imai Kanehira|1=Imai Kanehira}}
{{ill|Imai Nobuyoshi|ja|今井信是|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai Nobuyoshi|1=Imai Nobukore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17193538}}
{{ill|Imai Nobumoto|ja|今井信元|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai Nobumoto|1=Imai Nobumoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17193540}}
{{ill|Imai Nobutoshi|ja|今井信俊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai Nobutoshi|1=Imai Nobutoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q85876064}} }} '''Imai Clan''' (今井氏) is a Japanese {{ill|Clan|en|Clan|qid=Q211503|lt=clan|1=Clan}} with various origins. == Kai Province: Imai Clan == === Seiwa Genji Takeda Branch === The Imai Clan of {{ill|Kai Province|en|Kai Province|qid=Q858076|lt=Kai Province|1=Kai Province}}, based in Yamanashi District, was a cadet branch of the {{ill|Takeda clan|en|Takeda clan|qid=Q1210114|lt=Takeda Clan|1=Takeda clan}}, tracing its lineage to {{ill|Takeda Nobutake|ja|武田信満|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Nobutake|1=Takeda Nobumitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11546103}}'s son, {{ill|Takeda Nobukage|ja|武田信景|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Nobukage|1=Takeda Nobukage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11546101}}. The main branch, known as the Ura Imai Clan,"Ura Imai Clan" is also written as "Ura Imai" in some sources. See Marushima Kazuhiro, ‘Kōshin Region,’ in Oishi Yasufumi, ed., *Guide to Kokushu Nationwide,* Seikaisha Shinsho, 2015. acted as regional leaders during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|qid=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}. The clan rebelled multiple times against {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|en|Takeda Nobutora|qid=Q1192628|lt=Takeda Nobutora|1=Takeda Nobutora}} but eventually surrendered. Members such as {{ill|Imai Nobufusa|ja|今井信房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai Nobufusa|1=Imai Nobufusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q59552246}} and {{ill|Imai Nobutoshi|ja|今井信俊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai Nobutoshi|1=Imai Nobutoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q85876064}} later served as retainers to the Takeda family.Marushima Kazuhiro, ‘Kōshin Region,’ in Oishi Yasufumi, ed., *Guide to Kokushu Nationwide,* Seikaisha Shinsho, 2015. === Kai's Unification === In 1582, during the Takeda Clan's downfall, {{ill|Imai Nobutoshi|ja|今井信俊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai Nobutoshi|1=Imai Nobutoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q85876064}} switched allegiances to {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|qid=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}, securing the clan's survival. == Kōzuke Province: Imai Clan == === Nakahara Branch === The Imai Clan in Kōzuke traces its lineage to the Nakahara Clan from {{ill|Kiso|en|Kiso|qid=Q355726|lt=Kiso|1=Kiso}}. According to legends, retainers of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|en|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|qid=Q1139202|lt=Minamoto no Yoshinaka|1=Minamoto no Yoshinaka}} fled to Kiso after his death, founding the Imai lineage.Hokutachibana Village Historical Compilation Committee, *Hokutachibana Village Gazetteer,* 1975. == See Also == * {{ill|Igeta Crest|ja|唐花紋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igeta Crest|1=karahanamon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q21550705}} * {{ill|Fuchuin Temple (Kofu)|ja|冨春院 (甲府市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuchuin Temple (Kofu)|1=Fushun-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q35669644}} * {{ill|Chogenin Temple|ja|長源院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chogenin Temple|1=Chōgen-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q18233762}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} ==References== {{translated page|ja|今井氏|version=102959884|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:今井氏]] {{wikidata link|Q24871105}} [[Category:Clans of Chūbu Region]] [[Category:Clans of Kantō Region]] [[Category:Clans of Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Clans of Shinano Province]] [[Category:History of Hokuto, Yamanashi]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:History of Nagano]] [[Category:History of Ota, Gunma]] [[Category:History of Saku]] [[Category:History of Shibukawa, Gunma]] [[Category:Imai Clan of Kai Province]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Nakahara Clan of Kiso]] [[Category:Nitta clan]] [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
